description: Update E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md with comprehensive manual test flows for all new features: Recipe Saving, Personal Recipes, Recipe Sharing, RecipeHub subtab navigation, and Meal Planning

userStories.title: Add Recipe Saving E2E test flows (Section 15)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want manual E2E test flows for the recipe saving/bookmarking feature so that I can verify save/unsave functionality across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 15 'Recipe Saving' added to E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md after Section 14
Flow 15.1: Save a club recipe - click bookmark icon on RecipeCard in Club Recipes tab, verify filled purple bookmark appears, verify toast 'Recipe saved to collection'
Flow 15.2: Unsave a recipe - click filled bookmark icon, verify icon goes unfilled, verify toast 'Recipe removed from collection'
Flow 15.3: View saved recipes in My Recipes tab - save a club recipe, switch to 'My Recipes' subtab, verify saved recipe appears with ingredient tag and 'Saved' indicator
Flow 15.4: Bookmark not shown on personal recipes - add a personal recipe, verify no bookmark icon on it in My Recipes tab
Flow 15.5: Save persists across page navigation - save a recipe, navigate away and back to Recipes tab, verify recipe still shows as saved
Formatting matches existing E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md style (numbered steps, **Verify:** markers)
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Read src/components/recipes/SaveRecipeButton.tsx (63 lines) and src/components/recipes/RecipeHub.tsx (lines 385-395 for handleSaveToggle, lines 527-534 for RecipeCard rendering with isSaved prop). The bookmark icon is a Lucide Bookmark that shows filled purple when saved. saved_recipes table is a simple user_id + recipe_id junction.

userStories.title: Add Personal Recipes E2E test flows (Section 16)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want manual E2E test flows for personal recipe management so that I can verify adding, viewing, and managing personal recipes.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 16 'Personal Recipes' added to E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md after Section 15
Flow 16.1: Add personal recipe with name and URL - click 'My Recipes' subtab, click 'Add Recipe' button, fill name + URL, submit. Verify toast 'Personal recipe added!', recipe appears in list
Flow 16.2: Add personal recipe name only - submit with name only (no URL). Verify recipe created, no 'View recipe' link shown on card
Flow 16.3: URL validation - enter invalid URL (not starting with http/https), verify red border + error message 'URL must start with http:// or https://'
Flow 16.4: Personal badge - verify personal recipes show purple 'Personal' badge on RecipeCard
Flow 16.5: Empty state - with no personal or saved recipes, verify empty state message 'No personal recipes yet. Add one or save a club recipe!'
Flow 16.6: Cancel dialog - open Add Recipe dialog, type a name, click Cancel, reopen dialog, verify fields are cleared
Flow 16.7: Share personal recipe - verify Share button is available on personal recipes
Formatting matches existing E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md style
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Read src/components/recipes/AddPersonalRecipeDialog.tsx (145 lines). Creates recipe with event_id: null, ingredient_id: null. The 'Add Recipe' button only shows when subTab === 'personal' && userId. RecipeHub.tsx line 498-507 for the button. The dialog clears fields on close (onOpenChange handler lines 71-75). RecipeCard determines isPersonal based on recipe.isPersonal flag.

userStories.title: Add Recipe Sharing E2E test flows (Section 17)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want manual E2E test flows for recipe sharing so that I can verify the share dialog, email sending, shared-with-me view, and share-only user access.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 17 'Recipe Sharing' added to E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md after Section 16

Flow 17.1: Share recipe via dialog - click Share icon on any recipe card, verify dialog opens with recipe name in description, enter valid email + optional message, click Share. Verify toast 'Recipe shared with {email}!'
Flow 17.2: Email validation - type invalid email, verify red border + 'Please enter a valid email address', verify Share button disabled
Flow 17.3: Duplicate share prevention - share same recipe to same email twice, verify toast error 'This recipe has already been shared with this email'
Flow 17.4: View Shared with Me tab - switch to 'Shared with Me' subtab. Verify shared recipes displayed with: recipe name, 'Shared by {name}', personal message (if provided), date, 'Shared' badge
Flow 17.5: New badge on unviewed shares - verify unviewed shares show purple 'New' badge
Flow 17.6: Mark as viewed via View Recipe - click 'View Recipe' link on a shared recipe with URL. Verify 'New' badge disappears after returning to the tab
Flow 17.7: Mark as viewed button - for shares without URL, verify 'Mark as Viewed' button appears. Click it, verify 'New' badge disappears
Flow 17.8: Empty shared state - with no shares, verify empty state with Share2 icon and 'No recipes have been shared with you yet.'
Flow 17.9: Share-only user access - log in as share-only user (access_type='share_only'). Verify: only 'Shared with Me' subtab visible, no 'Club Recipes' or 'My Recipes' tabs, no Home/Events tabs
Flow 17.10: Dev mode sharing - in dev mode, share a recipe. Verify: direct DB insert (no edge function call), recipe share still created successfully
Formatting matches existing E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md style
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Read src/components/recipes/ShareRecipeDialog.tsx (174 lines) and src/components/recipes/SharedWithMeSection.tsx (191 lines). The ShareRecipeDialog has dev mode branch (lines 46-63) that inserts directly to recipe_shares instead of calling the edge function. SharedWithMeSection queries recipe_shares joined with recipes and profiles. The share-only user logic is in RecipeHub.tsx line 44 (defaults subTab to 'shared') and lines 425-444 (hides club/personal tabs). The 23505 error code is PostgreSQL unique violation.

userStories.title: Add RecipeHub Subtab Navigation E2E test flows (Section 18)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want manual E2E test flows for the RecipeHub subtab navigation so that I can verify switching between Club, My Recipes, and Shared with Me tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 18 'RecipeHub Subtab Navigation' added to E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md after Section 17
Flow 18.1: Default tab - navigate to Recipes tab on dashboard. Verify 'Club Recipes' subtab is active (purple background), club recipes displayed
Flow 18.2: Switch to My Recipes - click 'My Recipes' subtab. Verify: tab highlights purple, personal recipes + saved club recipes shown, 'Add Recipe' button visible, ingredient filter hidden
Flow 18.3: Switch to Shared with Me - click 'Shared with Me' subtab. Verify: tab highlights purple, shared recipes or empty state shown, search bar and ingredient filter hidden
Flow 18.4: Search across tabs - enter search term on Club tab, verify results filter. Switch to My Recipes, verify search still filters. Verify search works on recipe names and note content
Flow 18.5: Ingredient filter (Club only) - on Club tab, select ingredient from dropdown. Verify recipes filtered. Switch to My Recipes, verify ingredient filter is not shown
Flow 18.6: Loading states - switch tabs rapidly. Verify loading spinner appears briefly during data fetch
Formatting matches existing E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md style
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Read src/components/recipes/RecipeHub.tsx. Subtabs defined as type RecipeSubTab = 'club' | 'personal' | 'shared'. The subtab buttons are at lines 424-455. Ingredient filter only renders when subTab === 'club' (line 480). Search filters on recipe name + note text (lines 398-411). loadRecipes() switches between loadClubRecipes() and loadPersonalRecipes() based on subTab. Shared tab renders SharedWithMeSection directly.

userStories.title: Add Meal Planning E2E test flows (Section 19)
userStories.description: As a tester, I want manual E2E test flows for the meal planning system so that I can verify the weekly grid, meal CRUD, preferences, AI suggestions, and chat panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Section 19 'Meal Planning' added to E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md after Section 18

Flow 19.1: Navigate to Meal Plan tab - click 'Meals' tab on dashboard. Verify: 'Meal Plan' heading, 'Preferences' and 'Get Suggestions' buttons, week navigation, 7-day x 3-meal grid (breakfast/lunch/dinner)
Flow 19.2: Week navigation - click right arrow for next week. Verify dates update. Click left arrow for previous week. Verify dates update. Click 'Today' to return to current week
Flow 19.3: Add meal via empty slot - click an empty grid slot. Verify pending slot banner appears ('Adding meal for {Day} {mealType}'). Verify cancel link in banner works

Flow 19.4: Add AI suggestion to pending slot - with pending slot active, click 'Get Suggestions'. Click a suggestion card. Verify meal appears in the selected slot, pending banner disappears, toast 'Added "{name}" to plan'

Flow 19.5: Add suggestion without pending slot - without clicking a slot first, click a suggestion. Verify it goes to next available dinner slot. If all dinner slots filled, verify error toast 'All dinner slots are filled. Click an empty slot first.'
Flow 19.6: Remove meal - click the X button on an occupied slot. Verify meal removed, toast 'Meal removed', slot returns to empty state

Flow 19.7: User preferences dialog - click 'Preferences' button. Verify dialog with: dietary restrictions (multi-select), cuisine preferences (multi-select), disliked ingredients (multi-select), household size (number), cooking skill (beginner/intermediate/advanced), max cook time (minutes)
Flow 19.8: Save preferences - fill out preferences and save. Verify toast confirmation, preferences persist after closing/reopening dialog
Flow 19.9: AI suggestions panel - click 'Get Suggestions'. Verify 3 suggestion cards appear with: name, cuisine tag, time estimate, reason. Verify each has an 'Add to Plan' button

Flow 19.10: AI chat panel - type a message (e.g., 'I want something spicy') and press Enter/send. Verify: user message appears in chat, assistant response appears, new suggestions refresh based on request
Flow 19.11: Dev mode suggestions - in dev mode, click 'Get Suggestions'. Verify mock suggestions appear (Mediterranean Quinoa Bowl, Honey Garlic Salmon, One-Pot Pasta Primavera) without errors
Flow 19.12: Week persistence - add meals to a week, navigate away, navigate back to same week. Verify meals are still present
Flow 19.13: Loading state - on initial load, verify spinner displays while meal plan loads
Formatting matches existing E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md style
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Read src/components/mealplan/MealPlanPage.tsx (433 lines) for the main orchestrator. Key sub-components: WeekNavigation.tsx (prev/next/today buttons + date range display), MealPlanGrid.tsx (63 lines, 7 days x 3 meal types), MealPlanSlot.tsx (individual slot with add/remove), AISuggestionPanel.tsx (shows MealSuggestion cards), AIChatPanel.tsx (chat input + message history), PreferencesDialog.tsx (form with all preference fields). Dev mode mock suggestions are hardcoded at lines 279-301 of MealPlanPage.tsx. The grid shows DAY_LABELS = ['Sun','Mon','Tue','Wed','Thu','Fri','Sat'] and MEAL_TYPES = ['breakfast','lunch','dinner'] (no snack - only 3 meal types, not 4). The pendingSlot state (line 41) controls the 'Adding meal for...' banner.

userStories.title: Update environment notes and known issues for new features
userStories.description: As a tester, I want the Test Environment Notes and Known Issues sections updated so that the document reflects all new feature considerations.
acceptanceCriteria: Test Environment Notes section updated with: share-only user access type note, dev mode recipe sharing (direct DB insert vs edge function), meal plan AI suggestions (mock data in dev mode)
Known Issues table updated if any known issues exist for new features
Add note about feature flag or disabled features related to new features if any
Add note about share-only test user setup (access_type in allowed_users table)
Formatting matches existing E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md style
npm run build passes
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Read the bottom of E2E_TEST_FLOWS.md (lines 520-539) for the existing Known Issues and Test Environment Notes sections. Key new environment notes: (1) share-only users need access_type='share_only' in allowed_users table, (2) recipe sharing in dev mode bypasses edge function and inserts directly to recipe_shares, (3) meal plan AI suggestions return mock data in dev mode (see MealPlanPage.tsx lines 277-309), (4) meal plan grid uses 3 meal types (breakfast/lunch/dinner), not 4 as originally planned.
